data.title: Australia's Sun Silver gains on strong silver re-assay results
data.description:
** Shares of Sun Silver rise as much as 8.6% to A$1.075, their highest level since October 23, 2024

** Stock last up 7.1% at A$1.06

** The silver and gold explorer says significantly higher silver grades returned from re-assays at its Maverick Springs Project in Nevada

** Around 1.3 mln shares traded, 1.2x the 30-day avg

** YTD, up 69.6%, including current session's moves

data.title: Kingboard Laminates Parent to Sell Shares in Company; Shares Fall 10%
data.description:
Kingboard Laminates said its parent Kingboard Holdings agreed to sell shares of the company, according to a Hong Kong bourse filing Thursday.

Shares of Kingboard Laminates fell over 10% in morning trade Friday while Kingboard Holdings was down nearly 3%.

Kingboard Holdings is looking to issue 78.5 million shares of Kingboard Laminates at HK$10 apiece via its unit Kingboard Investments.

The sale will reduce Kingboard Holdings' ownership in Kingboard Laminates from 73.76% to 71.25%.

Kingboard Holdings hired CLSA to oversee the placement.

data.title: India's equity benchmarks open little changed as financials offset broader gains
data.description:
India's equity benchmarks opened flat on Friday, as a decline in financial stocks, led by Axis Bank after a quarterly profit miss, offset gains across other sectors.

The Nifty 50 was down 0.01% to 25,108.55 points, while the BSE Sensex lost 0.08% to 82,193.62, as of 9:15 a.m. IST.

Ten of the 13 major sectors logged gains at the open. However, the heaviest weighted financials and private banks (.NIFPVTBNK) lost 0.5% and 1.2%, respectively.

Axis Bank lost 6% after posting a surprise profit drop in the June quarter due to an increase in bad loans after a one-time industry benchmarking exercise. It was the top Nifty 50 loser.

The broader, more domestically focussed small-caps and mid-caps were flat.

Asian stocks rose on the day, after data showed U.S. retail sales rose after two months of decline in June, while fewer-than-expected jobless claims were filed last week, indicating resilience in the world's largest economy, which boosted investor confidence globally.

data.title: Technip Energies, Shell Unit Partner to Scale Carbon Capture for Industries
data.description:
Technip Energies signed a global alliance agreement with Shell Catalysts & Technologies to develop carbon capture delivery technology using Shell's Cansolv1 CO2 Capture System.

The French technology and engineering company said Thursday its collaboration with the oil and gas giant's unit aims to deliver a post-combustion amine-based carbon capture system for the industrial sectors.

data.title: Subsea 7 Secures Contract for Equinor's Norwegian Offshore Project
data.description:
Subsea 7 secured an engineering, procurement, construction and installation contract for the Fram Sør development project offshore Norway from Norwegian oil and gas company Equinor .

The subsea engineering and construction company will provide engineering, project management, and installation of subsea structures and production, gas lift, and water injection lines spanning 53 kilometers, along with an umbilical system, according to a Thursday release.

Subsea 7 will start engineering and project management immediately in its Norway and UK offices, while offshore installation is anticipated to commence in 2026, 2027, and 2028.

data.title: Longchen Paper's Board OKs Nankan Plant Asset Review; Shares Rally 7%
data.description:
Longchen Paper & Packaging's board approved the initiation of an asset revitalization assessment for its Nankan plant in Taiwan, according to a Thursday Taiwan Exchange filing.

Shares jumped nearly 7% in late-morning trade Friday.

The decision follows interest from potential buyers and the completion of automation and smart upgrades at the nearby Longtan Plant in December 2021.

The company said it can now handle orders at the Nankan plant, thanks to a 66% capacity boost at its Longtan facility — from 90 million to 150 million square meters annually.

data.title: TSMC Shares on Track for Record Close After Earnings Beat
data.description:
By Sherry Qin

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.'s shares are on track to close at a record high after it delivered an earnings beat and raised revenue guidance for 2025 despite multiple headwinds.

Shares of the world's largest contract chip maker in Taipei were up 2.2% Friday morning at 1,155 New Taiwan Dollars, equivalent to US$39.25. TSMC's shares have climbed steadily over the past three months and are set to surpass the previous high reached in January. Shares had taken a hit earlier this year amid concerns about the sustainability of AI spending and U.S. President Trump's tariff policy.

The company on Thursday reported a 61% jump in second-quarter net profit from a year earlier to 398.27 billion New Taiwan dollars, equivalent to US$13.57 billion, topping market expectations. Its revenue rose 39% to NT$933.79 billion.

The chip maker raised its guidance for 2025 revenue growth to around 30% from a mid-20% rise projected earlier despite headwinds from U.S. tariffs and unfavorable foreign exchange rates.

According to analysts, TSMC's commanding lead in chip making and advanced packaging technology should help sustain its industry leadership and long-term growth.

Management highlighted in a post-earnings call on Thursday that the capacity for both advanced chips and advanced packaging remains tight versus demand and it is trying to narrow the gap.

Its next-generation 2-nanometer chips, scheduled for mass production later this year, would require substantial manufacturing complexity and highly refined 3D process control, Citi analysts said in a research note.

TSMC's long-standing expertise in process control, which is one of its core competitive advantages, should support selling its value, the analysts added.

Meanwhile, DBS analyst Jim Au said the market hasn't fully appreciated TSMC's advanced packaging technology, which could become a major profit driver in the coming years. As chips grow smaller, finding smarter ways to integrate different types of chips has become a more important way to improve performance.

While U.S. tariff policy uncertainties have clouded the company's outlook, TSMC chief executive C.C. Wei has downplayed the tariff impact on its business. On Thursday, he reiterated that the company hadn't seen any changes in customers' behavior.

"TSMC is undervalued as the market is overestimating tariff effects and underestimating the longevity of AI investments," Morningstar analyst Phelix Lee said, adding that Taiwan could reach a trade deal with the U.S. before the August 1 deadline.

Write to Sherry Qin at sherry.qin@wsj.com

data.title: RBA Will Face Heated Scrutiny Again Next Week — Market Talk
data.description: The week ahead will be a tough one for the Reserve Bank of Australia, which is in the spotlight for failing to cut interest rates this month, despite economists arguing strongly that the case for easing was there for all to see. RBA Gov. Michele Bullock will speak publicly Thursday. Since the RBA kept rates on hold, data from the job market has shown deterioration, raising expectations the RBA will cut in August. Still, Bullock is skilled in calming press packs. She will put her defense and likely point toward likely cuts in the very near future. (james.glynn@wsj.com; X @JamesGlynnWSJ)

data.title: Global Forex and Fixed Income Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering FX and Fixed Income. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0354 GMT - The week ahead will be a tough one for the Reserve Bank of Australia, which is in the spotlight for failing to cut interest rates this month, despite economists arguing strongly that the case for easing was there for all to see. RBA Gov. Michele Bullock will speak publicly Thursday. Since the RBA kept rates on hold, data from the job market has shown deterioration, raising expectations the RBA will cut in August. Still, Bullock is skilled in calming press packs. She will put her defense and likely point toward likely cuts in the very near future. (james.glynn@wsj.com; X @JamesGlynnWSJ)

0344 GMT - Singapore's strong export performance in June is unlikely to continue in 2H, CGS International economists write in a note. Global demand is likely to remain weak, they say. As a trade-dependent economy, Singapore is particularly vulnerable to external shocks. Its exports could be indirectly affected by tariff uncertainties involving its key trading partners such as China. Sector-specific tariffs could also further weigh on non-oil domestic exports, the economists say. CGS International maintains its 2025 NODX growth forecast for Singapore at 3.3%.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0301 GMT - Singapore's adoption of artificial intelligence could help sustain GDP growth of around 3% in the medium term and unlock further productivity, Morgan Stanley analysts and economists write in a note. The city-state is among the top AI markets globally. AI powers many public services in Singapore, including chronic health management systems in hospitals and adaptive learning systems in schools, they say. Morgan Stanley sees Singtel taking the lead in building AI infrastructure, noting that it is expanding its data-center capacity in Singapore.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0257 GMT - The Singapore dollar strengthens against its U.S. counterpart in the Asian session amid risk-on sentiment spurred by mild gains in U.S. stock-index futures. On the technical chart, USD/SGD's recent "whipsawing price action has resulted in a mixed outlook," Quek Ser Leang of UOB's Global Economics & Markets Research says. For today, the currency pair is most likely to trade in a range between 1.2825 and 1.2875, the senior technical strategist says in a research report. USD/SGD is 0.2% lower at 1.2839. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0144 GMT - The Philippines stands a good chance of lowering its U.S. reciprocal tariff to the 10% baseline, says Darren Tay, head of Asia country risk at BMI, a unit of Fitch Solutions. "While Trump has raised the full reciprocal rate from 17% to 20% for no explicit reason, the Philippines still faces some of the lowest tariffs in Asia," Tay says at a webinar. There have been few details from bilateral trade talks, but Tay reckons that defense spending would be a point of contention. Making concessions on defense is a good way for the Philippines to secure a trade deal, given that one of Trump's priorities is pushing allies to do more, Tay says. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0130 GMT - The yen is poised to stay under pressure in the near term, TD Securities strategists say in a research report. Surveys suggest Japan's ruling coalition won't be able to win over 50 seats in Sunday's Upper House elections to keep its simple majority, the strategists say. FX options indicate rising bearishness toward JPY as markets price in election and fiscal deterioration risks, the strategists note. "If PM Ishiba decides to resign on an election loss, USD/JPY could easily break above 149.70 as it would usher in an initial period of political turbulence," the strategists add. USD/JPY is 0.1% lower at 148.50. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0101 GMT - The Philippine central bank is expected to deliver three rate cuts by year-end, J.P. Morgan economists write in a note. The impact of U.S. tariffs is limited given the country's small trade exposure, they say. Trump has recently raised the Philippine's tariff rate to 20% from 17%. Domestic demand remains weak, the economists say, and the BSP's rate cuts so far has been unable to revive credit demand due to factors such as supply overhang in the residential property market. Inflation is also likely to remain below-target through 1Q 2026.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0034 GMT - Asian currencies consolidate against the dollar in the early Asian session amid mixed signals. On the one hand, stronger-than-expected U.S. economic data released overnight could dent Fed rate-cut expectations, thereby supporting the greenback. However, "USD remains vulnerable to the downside if concerns about U.S. policymaking further undermine investor confidence in USD assets," CBA's Carol Kong says in a research report. The dollar's weakness earlier this week due to worries over Fed Chair Powell's possible dismissal by President Trump is a "case in point," the economist and currency strategist says. USD/CNH is little changed at 7.1823; USD/TWD is steady at 29.43. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0017 GMT - Japan's underlying inflation is still elevated, and is almost certain to overshoot BOJ's forecasts, Capital Economics' Abhijit Surya says in commentary. The country's inflation excluding fresh food and energy rose 3.4% on year in June, up from the 3.3% increase in May, the senior APAC economist notes. "There continues to be a compelling case for the BOJ to resume its tightening cycle later this year," Surya says. However, U.S.-Japan tariff negotiations remain "up in the air," so there's a growing risk that the BOJ will delay its next rate increase until early 2026, Surya adds. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0014 GMT - Japanese stocks are higher following signs of U.S. economic strength. Tech and financial stocks are leading gains. Nomura Research Institute is up 1.0% and Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group is 0.6% higher. USD/JPY is at 148.47, compared with 148.73 as of Thursday's Tokyo stock market close. Investors are focusing on any developments related to U.S. tariffs and domestic economic policy ahead of the Upper House elections on Sunday. The Nikkei Stock Average is up 0.2% at 39968.38. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

0010 GMT - JGBs consolidate ahead of Japan's Upper House elections on Sunday. Surveys surrounding the elections show prospects of the Liberal Democratic Party-Komeito coalition retaining its majority have grown even more uncertain than earlier in the campaign, two members of Barclays' FICC Research say in a research report. "If the fiscally dovish opposition parties fare even better than projected in the pre-election polls, the initial post-election response would likely be an accelerated bear-steepening in JGBs," the members say. The 10-year JGB yield is steady at 1.555%. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

2342 GMT - Japanese stocks may rise following signs of U.S. economic strength. Nikkei futures are up 0.4% at 40050 on the SGX. USD/JPY is at 148.31, compared with 148.73 as of Thursday's Tokyo stock market close. Investors are focusing on any developments related to U.S. tariffs and domestic economic policy ahead of the Upper House election on Sunday. The Nikkei Stock Average rose 0.6% to 39901.19 on Thursday. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com)

data.title: Ace Pillar OKs Capital Increase for Unit; Shares Rally 8%
data.description:
Ace Pillar approved a capital increase for its unit, Transpak Equipment, issuing over 8.8 million new shares by capitalizing surplus, according to a Thursday Taiwan Exchange filing.

Shares rose 8% recently on Friday.

Shareholders will receive 7.478 shares for every 1,000 held.

The move aims to strengthen Transpak Equipment's capital structure, it said.

data.title: Public Financial Books H1 Profit; Shares Gain 2%
data.description:
Public Financial reported HK$2.6 million in attributable profit for the six months ended June 30, compared to a loss of HK$34.5 million, according to a Hong Kong bourse filing Thursday.

Shares of the bank fell over 2% in morning trade Friday.

Diluted earnings per share were HK$0.002 compared to a loss of HKR0.031 in the corresponding period of 2024.

The money lender's net interest income was HK$530.5 million, higher than the HK$470.7 million a year ago.

data.title: India stock exchange derivative activity slips by a third since Jane Street trading ban
data.description:
Trading in India's weekly equity index options has slumped by a third since the country's market regulator banned U.S. high-frequency trading giant Jane Street in the local market earlier this month, exchange data showed on Thursday.

National Stock Exchange of India - the world's largest derivatives exchange by number of contracts traded - saw a nearly 36% drop over two weeks in index options premium turnover, a key measure of real capital deployed and risk appetite.

The options premium turnover stood at 396.26 billion rupees ($4.6 billion) on Thursday, which is the day of weekly options expiry on NSE.

The Securities and Exchange Board of India barred Jane Street on July 4, saying an investigation found it manipulated stock indexes through positions taken in derivatives.

NSE's rival exchange BSE also saw its options premium turnover drop 36.4% below the July 3 levels. BSE index options expire on Tuesdays.

Emails to NSE and BSE were not immediately answered.

Out of the 10 sessions since the ban, turnover has declined in six on a week-on-week basis across both the exchanges.

"The notable decline in options premium turnover can be attributed to the abrupt withdrawal or reduction of activity by Jane Street, which serves as a primary liquidity provider within the options market," said Osho Krishan, senior analyst of technical and derivatives research at brokerage Angel One.

Unless new market-makers step in or volatility rises materially, turnover is unlikely to bounce back soon, Krishan said.

Traders also point to a broader lull in volatility dragging volumes.

"This isn't just a Jane Street story," said Mayank Bansal, a portfolio manager in India's options market. "It's mostly about volatility — once that comes back, so will the volumes."

The Nifty volatility index has fallen in nine of the 13 sessions in July so far, and was hovering near a more than one-year low.

($1 = 86.0410 Indian rupees)

data.title: Metals Australia jumps on increased presence of graphite resource at Canada project
data.description:
** Metals Australia jumps as much as 17.7% to A$0.020; set for best session since November 8, 2024

** Stock hits highest since May 27

** Miner finds ~595% surge in presence of graphitic carbon resources at its Lac Carheil project in Quebec

** Says drilling results expected to support multi-decade exploration at site

** Stock sees most active session since December 9, 2024, with ~3.8 mln shares traded - ~11x the 30-day avg

** MLS last up 14.7%, trimming YTD losses to 15.2%

data.title: China Resources Pharma Plans 1 Billion Yuan Innovation Fund; Shares Gain 1%
data.description:
China Resources Pharmaceutical plans to form a 1 billion yuan innovation-focused fund based in Chengdu with other group subsidiaries and external parties, according to a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

Shares of the company were up nearly 1% in Friday morning trade.

The fund, tentatively called China Resources Pharmaceutical (Chengdu) Innovation Investment Fund, will have a seven-year term and focus on equity investments in innovative drugs, vaccines, traditional Chinese medicine, high-end medical devices, and other strategic sectors.

China Resources Pharma and its units, including CR Boya Bio-pharmaceutical, Dong-E-E-Jiao , Double-Crane , and Jiangzhong Pharmaceutical , are expected to contribute a combined 245 million yuan, or 24.5% of total commitments.

Shenzhen China Resources Capital Equity Investment is expected to manage the fund.

data.title: Vietnam warns of food supply disruptions as African swine fever spreads
data.description:
African swine fever outbreaks are spreading in Vietnam, threatening to disrupt food supplies in the Southeast Asian country, the government warned on Friday.

Vietnam has this year detected 514 outbreaks in 28 out of 34 cities and provinces nationwide, the government said in a statement, adding that the authorities have culled more than 30,000 infected pigs.

"The risk of African swine fever is on a rising trend, negatively affecting the pig farming industry, food supplies and the environment," the government said.

African swine fever has disrupted the global pork market for years. In the worst outbreak over 2018-19, about half the domestic pig population died in China, the world's biggest producer, causing losses estimated at over $100 billion.

The recent outbreaks in Vietnam have prompted Prime Minister Pham Minh Chinh to send an urgent directive to provinces and government agencies this week to deploy measures to curb the disease.

Vietnam in 2023 approved the domestic commercial use of its first home-grown African swine fever vaccines, but officials said the rate of vaccinated pigs remains low.

"Only around 30% of the pigs in my province have been vaccinated," said an animal health official of Quang Ngai province, where infections have been reported over the past few weeks.

"It's not clear why the rate is low - it could either be the issue of vaccine availability, efficiency or cost," said another provincial official, who declined to be named as the person is not authorised to speak to the media.

The agriculture ministry's Department of Animal Health didn't respond to Reuters' request for comment. Calls to AVAC Vietnam JSC, the country's main African swine fever vaccine producer, went unanswered.

AVAC said last month it had sold 3 million vaccine doses in the domestic market and exported 600,000 doses to the Philippines and Indonesia.

data.title: India's Wipro climbs after quarterly earnings beat estimates
data.description: India's fourth-largest IT company, Wipro climbed 3.5% in early trade on Friday, after it posted better-than-expected quarterly earnings on improved client spending in parts of its Americas business.

data.title: Dalian iron ore set for fourth weekly gain on demand optimism
data.description:
Dalian iron ore futures climbed on Friday and were headed for a fourth consecutive weekly gain, as upbeat demand outlook and hopes for further policy support from Beijing boosted market sentiment.

The most-traded September iron ore contract on China's Dalian Commodity Exchange (DCE) rose 0.7% to 787.5 yuan ($109.67) a metric ton, as of 0320 GMT.

The contract has gained 3.01% this week.

The benchmark August iron ore (SZZFQ5) on the Singapore Exchange was 0.57% higher at $101.4 a ton, up 2.19% this week.

Traders are optimistic about improving steel margins following Beijing's signals to tackle over-capacity, while hopes for new property-sector stimulus have further lifted market sentiment, analysts from ANZ said in a note.

Lower inventories for both iron ore and steel are also fuelling expectations of restocking in the months ahead, ANZ added.

Total stockpiles of iron ore across ports in China fell 0.76% week-on-week to 130.9 million tons as of July 18, according to consultancy SteelHome data, lending further support to prices.

Meanwhile, current demand for iron ore is resilient, and steel consumption in the manufacturing sector remains at a high level, said broker Galaxy Futures.

Expectations of supply-side policy reforms are also providing further support to prices, Galaxy added.

Elsewhere, mining giant BHP reported a record annual iron ore production of 290 million tons, with fourth-quarter output of 77.5 million tons exceeding market expectations.

The dollar rallied on the back of strong retail sales data.

A stronger greenback makes dollar-denominated assets more expensive for holders of other currencies.

Other steelmaking ingredients on the DCE rose, with coking coal NYMEX:ACT1! and coke (DCJcv1) up 2.55% and 0.8%, respectively.

Steel benchmarks on the Shanghai Futures Exchange all gained ground. Rebar and wire rod (SWRcv1) increased 0.83%, hot-rolled coil was up 0.88%, and stainless steel strengthened 0.24%.

($1 = 7.1807 Chinese yuan)

data.title: India's Axis Bank slumps as first-quarter results disappoint
data.description:
India's Axis Bank shed 4.6% on Friday, a day after the private lender reported an unexpected drop in June quarter profit, with analysts citing higher credit costs and margin pressures as key concerns.

The stock was the top loser on Nifty Bank and Nifty Private Bank (.NIFPVTBNK), which were trading 0.4% and 0.9% lower, respectively.

It also fell the most on benchmark Nifty 50 , which was trading flat.

India's fourth-largest private lender by market value reported a surprise lower quarterly profit drop on Thursday, as its bad loans surged after a one-time industry benchmarking exercise. Its net interest margin also contracted to 3.8% from 4.05%.

The bank's new asset recognition norms weighed on asset quality, with high credit costs adversely impacting the profit, BOBCaps analysts said.

data.title: [LIVE] Crypto News Today: Latest Updates for July 18, 2025 –XRP Hits All-Time High Above $3.6, ETH   Trades at $3.6K as Crypto Bills Clear House
data.description:
The global crypto market is in full bull mode, total capitalization has surged past the $4 trillion threshold, led by a fresh wave of altcoin strength. XRP surged past its all-time high above $3.6 today. ETH is up nearly 8% in the past 24 hours, trading near $3,600. Bitcoin also broke above $120k barrier. Fueling the rally are major developments in Washington: the U.S. House has just cleared three critical crypto bills, including the GENIUS Act regulating stablecoins with the Senate having already passed key measures, pushing the legislative package toward President Trump’s desk.

But what else is happening in crypto news today? Follow our up-to-date live coverage below.

https://cryptonews.com/tags/bitcoin/

data.title: BHP Group Provides FY26 Guidance for Copper, Steelmaking Coal, Iron Ore Production
data.description:
BHP Group (, BHG.JO) on Friday laid out guidance for copper, steelmaking coal, energy coal, and iron ore production for fiscal 2026.

The mining giant expects copper production to range between 1.8 million tonnes and 2 million tonnes for fiscal 2026, compared with 2 million tonnes in the year ended June 30, 2025.

Steelmaking coal production is expected between 18 million tonnes and 20 million tonnes, compared with 18 million tonnes in fiscal 2025. Energy coal production is expected between 14 million tonnes and 16 million tonnes, compared with 15 million tonnes in fiscal 2025.

Iron ore production is expected between 258 million tonnes and 269 million tonnes, compared with 263 million tonnes in fiscal 2025.

data.title: Datang International Power Generation's On-Grid Power Generation Rises 1.3% in H1
data.description:
Datang International Power Generation's (, SHA:60199) on-grid power generation in the first half rose 1.3% year on year to almost 124 billion kilowatt hours, according to a Shanghai bourse filing on Friday.

The rise was attributed to an increase in on-grid hydropower, wind power and photovoltaic power generation. The on-grid coal-fired and gas turbine power generation both fell.

The power company's average on-grid tariff during the period dropped 4% year on year to 444.48 yuan per megawatt hour, while market-based electricity volume jumped 87% to 107.7 billion kWh from a year earlier.

data.title: India's Sensex Opens 0.1% Lower at 82193.62, Dragged by Bank Stocks
Telia Signs MoU to Sell Latvian Asset Stakes
data.description:
Telia Co. signed a memorandum of understanding to sell its Latvian operations to the Republic of Latvia, Latvenergo and LVRTC, according to a Thursday release.

The telecoms operator owns a 49% stake in fixed network operator Tet and a 60.3% stake in mobile network operator LMT. The parties intend to finalize the deal by the end of 2025, with closing expected in the first half of 2026.

data.title: Hengrui Medicine Gets Approval to Start Cancer Clinical Trials
data.description:
Jiangsu Hengrui Medicine received regulatory approval from China's drug regulator to begin clinical trials for four drugs targeting various cancers.

SHR-8068 is a CTLA-4 antibody imported by the drugmaker, while Adevbelimab is an in-house PD-L1 therapy already approved domestically, according to a Friday filing with the Shanghai bourse. Bevacizumab is a biosimilar of Roche's Avastin, and Apatinib is a targeted therapy developed by the company.

Shares of Hengrui Medicine rose 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Posco Future M Widens Loss in Q2
data.description:
Posco Future M posted a net loss attributable to shareholders of the parent company of 35.6 billion won in the second quarter, widening from 8.74 billion won a year earlier, according to a Friday filing with the Korea Exchange.

Sales declined 27.8% to 660.9 billion won from 915.5 billion won during the period.

data.title: Kingston Resources Reprices Warrants Issued to Nominees
data.description:
Kingston Resources adjusted the exercise price of several classes of warrants issued to nominees Pure Asset Management and Horley, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The company repriced 25 million units of its warrants expiring July 7, 2027, to AU$0.0816 from AU$0.20, the filing said.

The company also repriced 35.7 million units of warrants expiring June 29, 2028, to AU$0.0816 from AU$0.14 and 69.7 million units of warrants expiring Feb. 23, 2028, to AU$0.0879 from AU$0.118, the filing said.

Shares of the company rose nearly 2% in recent Friday trade.

data.title: Saturn Metals' Western Australia Gold Project Mineral Resource Update Shows 59% Rise in Measured, Indicated Resource Category
data.description:
Saturn Metals said the measured, indicated, and inferred mineral resource estimate for the Apollo Hill gold project in Western Australia increased to 137.1 million tonnes at 0.51 grams per tonne grade of gold for 2.2 million ounces, representing a 59% increase in the combined measured and indicated mineral resource classification to 1.8 million ounces, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

The combined measured and indicated mineral resource classification represents 82% of the total mineral resource.

An additional 300 reverse circulation drill holes, nine diamond drill holes, as well as 25 additional metallurgical and geotechnical diamond drill holes holes contributed to the update.

The firm will use the updated estimate to develop the prefeasibility study, mining inventory, and production forecast as well as the first Apollo Hill ore reserve. Both are planned to be released later this year.

It is continuing to drill towards developing another mineral resource update, which will provide the basis for a definitive feasibility study, mining inventory, and production forecast, as well as an updated ore reserve.

data.title: Momentum for Singapore's Exports Could Face Headwinds — Market Talk
data.description: Singapore's strong export performance in June is unlikely to continue in 2H, CGS International economists write in a note. Global demand is likely to remain weak, they say. As a trade-dependent economy, Singapore is particularly vulnerable to external shocks. Its exports could be indirectly affected by tariff uncertainties involving its key trading partners such as China. Sector-specific tariffs could also further weigh on non-oil domestic exports, the economists say. CGS International maintains its 2025 NODX growth forecast for Singapore at 3.3%.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: Update: BHP Group's Copper Production Rises 2% in Fiscal Q4; Shares Up 3%
data.description:
(Updates to add BHP's stock movement in the headline and last paragraph)

BHP Group reported copper production of 516,200 tonnes in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2025, up 2% year on year and 1% sequentially, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

Iron ore output was 70.3 million tonnes, up 2% from a year earlier and 14% from the fiscal third quarter, the filing said.

In fiscal 2025, the company produced 2 million tonnes of copper and 263 million tonnes of iron ore, up 8% and 1%, respectively, from a year earlier.

For fiscal 2026, BHP said it expects copper production in the range of 1.8 million to 2 million tonnes and iron ore output of 258 million to 269 million tonnes, the filing added.

Shares of the company rose nearly 3% in recent Friday trade.

data.title: IVD Medical Plans Web3 Drug-Asset Exchange to Launch Stablecoin
data.description:
IVD Medical said it was pursuing a web3 exchange focused on tokenizing its intellectual property and planned to issue its own cryptocurrency, according to a Hong Kong bourse filing Thursday.

Shares of the medical equipment seller jumped over 7% in morning trade Friday.

The company said it planned to build a so-called real-world asset tokenization trading platform based on its drug pipeline, and that the IVDD stablecoin will serve as the transaction medium for trading the tokenized drug assets.

The company is preparing to seek regulatory approval in Hong Kong for a stablecoin license as well as in the U.S. where it also plans to seek a share listing.
